title: 'Love is sacrificing everything' — Dec 2021 aphorism
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-line Apple Note from Dec 22, 2021 (Yahir age 19, freshman fall at OSU just ended). Title is the thesis: "Love is sacrificing everything to make others better off." Body is the counter-weight: "Good intentions are the path to hell." The whole note is the dialectic.
  • Pure manifesto style note form — Yahir is rehearsing the love-as-output, not love-as-feeling frame three weeks after the child safety pitch fragment and during the same chunk where lynchpin life companion is being seeded. Sits philosophically next to the later debt of gratitude page — both treat loved people as obligations one acts on rather than feels toward.
  • The "good intentions = hell" half is unattributed but tracks the proverb (often credited to St. Bernard, popularised by Samuel Johnson). Yahir is using it to check the title — sacrifice without delivered improvement is failure. This is the same logic that later hardens into disconnected from outcome (positive form) and no runway discipline (negative form).
  • Read against the 2021 12 04 capitalisms climate essay seven days earlier and the Nov 22 metaverse land aphorism — late 2021 is Yahir's densest one-line thesis season in the corpus.
  • The pairing also previews the much later "stop observing, start loving" frame — same instinct (love is verb, not noun), four years apart.

Key quote

"Love is sacrificing everything to make others better off — Good intentions are the path to hell."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Explicit articulation of the value system that quietly drives the family as hidden runway arc — Yahir's later financial sacrifices for mom / siblings are the practical translation of this aphorism.
  • Anchors a concept stub: love-as-delivered-improvement (created below).

Open questions

  • Is this Yahir's own composition or a quote from a Dec-2021 source (book / podcast)? No surrounding context in the note.
